triage.clinical_question: Is the subtherapeutic valproate level of 34 mcg/mL intentional, and has there been any recent dose change, nonadherence, missed refills, or clinical change requiring adjustment?
decision_factors: Patient identifier is present and the valproate result is laboratory-verified.
Measured valproate level is 34 mcg/mL, below the supplied low threshold of 50 mcg/mL.
Current level is decreased from the listed baseline of 68 mcg/mL, suggesting a meaningful downward change.
No repeat reading, adverse symptom data, or concerning trend is provided to indicate immediate danger.

retrieval_query.clinical_question: Review the low laboratory-verified valproate level and determine whether it reflects an intentional regimen, adherence issue, recent dose change, or need for follow-up adjustment.

relevant_notes.title: Neurology follow-up
relevant_notes.text: No reported seizure activity. Continue current valproate dose and monitor levels.

missing_data: No documentation of intentional target valproate range below standard therapeutic range.
No note indicating recent dose change, missed refills, or nonadherence.
No medication administration/refill history or adherence assessment in retrieved chunks.
No more recent neurology follow-up than 2026-02-19 to clarify whether dose adjustment was considered after 2026-05-10 lab.

brief.alert_summary: Laboratory-verified valproate level is low at 34 mcg/mL, below the supplied threshold of 50 mcg/mL and lower than the prior baseline of 68 mcg/mL. Current evidence does not show seizures, new neurologic complaints, or a documented intentional low target, so clinician review is warranted, but there is no indication of immediate escalation from the provided data. [rpm:P005:alert:A005, ehr:P005:lab:1, ehr:P005:note:1, anam:P005:symptoms:1]

She reports taking doses as prescribed and was surprised by the low result. [ehr:P005:problem:1, ehr:P005:med:1, ehr:P005:lab:1, anam:P005:symptoms:1, anam:P005:adherence:1, anam:P005:concern:1]

contextual_analysis.text: The RPM alert shows a laboratory-verified valproate level of 34 mcg/mL, below the provided low threshold of 50 mcg/mL and below baseline 68 mcg/mL, supporting a meaningful downward change. [rpm:P005:alert:A005]
EHR data confirm an active valproate prescription at 500 mg twice daily and a sub-therapeutic lab value of 34 mcg/mL on 2026-05-10. [ehr:P005:med:1, ehr:P005:lab:1]
Neurology follow-up previously advised continuing the current valproate dose and monitoring levels, which does not explain whether the current low level was intended. [ehr:P005:note:1]

Patient-reported adherence is reassuring: she denies missed doses and reports no new supplements or major diet changes, while also reporting no seizure symptoms or new neurologic complaints. [anam:P005:adherence:1, anam:P005:lifestyle:1, anam:P005:symptoms:1]

risk_assessment.text: Persistently low valproate exposure could reduce seizure prophylaxis effectiveness if the level is not intentionally targeted. [ehr:P005:lab:1, ehr:P005:problem:1]
There is no current symptom evidence of breakthrough seizures or acute neurologic deterioration, which lowers concern for immediate harm from the alert alone. [anam:P005:symptoms:1, ehr:P005:note:1]

The record does not document intentional sub-therapeutic targeting, dose changes, refill problems, or nonadherence beyond the patient's self-report, so the reason for the low level remains uncertain. [ehr:P005:lab:1, ehr:P005:med:1, ehr:P005:note:1, anam:P005:adherence:1]
